title: RESOLVEENTITY
text: RESOLVEENTITY Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters external DTD declarations and unrecognized XML entity references. It must return the text or DTD to be substituted at the reference location. Explain this code operation RESOLVEENTITY params string PublicID: in string SystemID: in string ResolveIdentity: out endparams Implement your code here end; RESOLVEENTITY operation Copy Parameters PublicID —XML entity reference or DTD name SystemID —DTD file name or HTTP address ResolveIdentity —text to be substituted for the XML entity or the contents of the referenced DTD XML Entity References XML entities are symbolical names for pieces of XML or DTD that are defined in DTDs using the !ENTITY or !ENTITIES keywords. Once defined, XML entities can be referenced in an XML document using the & xxx ; syntax, where xxx is the name of the entity. When parsed, the parser substitutes the entity. However, when the parser encounters an XML entity reference it does not recognize, it activates RESOLVEENTITY . The operation must return the XML or DTD text that the parser should substitute at that position. For example, the following XML will causes RESOLVEENTITY to be activated: <abc> &def; </abc> Copy The operation should return some XML text in the ResolveIdentity parameter. For example: <def>texttext</def> Copy The parser then continues as if the XML document contained <def>texttext</def> at the &def; position. DTD Declarations RESOLVEENTITY is also activated when a <!DOCTYPE name… > declaration refers to an external DTD. In this case, the RESOLVEENTITY is called with the name as PublicID , and the DTD file name or http: address as SystemID . The operation must then return the content of the DTD file indicated in SystemID , or return an empty string. If an empty string is returned, the parser attempts to resolve the system ID itself by treating it as a URI. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
